ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT WAS DISMISSED MAY 24, 2016. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1, 2, 3 AND 4 AND GRANTED AS TO ISSUE 5. AS TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ISSUES 3 AND 4 (NEGLIGENCE II, LOSS OF CONSORTIUM), DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF NEGATING AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. DEFENDANT ASSERTS THIS IS THE ONLY WAY IT ATTEMPTED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN. WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH MR. THOMAS' DEPOSITION TESTIMONY, MR. TRUEBLOOD'S DECLARATION DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT NOR CAL EMPLOYEES NEVER WORKED WITH, CUT, INSTALLED AND/OR CLEANED UP ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PIPE INSULATION IN THE PRESENCE OF MR. THOMAS. AS TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE I) AND 2 (STRICT LIABILITY), DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT AS PLAINTIFFS' DID NOT ALLEGE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT. AS TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ISSUE 5 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES), DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS DID ASSERT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST DEFENDANT. (COMPLAINT, 56:23-26.) THE MOTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFFS' REPRESENTATIONS THAT THIS INCLUSION WAS INADVERTENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: MICHELE LUCAS, CSR 4017 (AIKEN WELCH, 510.451.1580)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THIS MATTER IS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. AS TO DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT REGARDING EXPOSURE, THE COURT INTERPRETS DEFENDANT'S REPLY AS WITHDRAWING THIS ISSUE. HOWEVER, IF DEFENDANT IS MOVING FORWARD WITH THIS ISSUE, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN. DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT IT SUBMITTED "AFFIRMATIVE EVIDENCE THAT CAMPBELL NEVER EXPOSED PLAINTIFF HOWARD THOMAS TO ASBESTOS." THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. THOMAS ON WHICH DEFENDANT RELIES IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT CAMPBELL EMPLOYEES DID NOT EXPOSE MR. THOMAS TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS. FINALLY, EVEN IF DEFENDANT DID SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN, MR. BAKER'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER CAMPBELL EMPLOYEES EXPOSED MR. THOMAS TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PIPE INSULATION. AS TO DEFENDANT'S DUTY ARGUMENT, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF NEGATING THAT ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. THOMAS AND THE DECLARATION OF BOB COOK DO NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT OWE A DUTY TO MR. THOMAS. THEY DO NOT SHOW THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT KNOW AND COULD NOT HAVE REASONABLY KNOWN THAT ITS EMPLOYEES' ACTIONS OF SWEEPING UP/CLEANING UP DUST AND DEBRIS COULD POSE A HEALTH HAZARD TO SOMEONE IN THE VICINITY OF THAT WORK. HOWEVER EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN, THE DECLARATION OF DR. RICHARD COHEN AND THE SAFETY ORDERS WOULD CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO MR. THOMAS. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF BOB COOK ARE OVERRULED. IF THE COURT GETS TO THE POINT OF CONSIDERING PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE, DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO DR. COHEN'S DECLARATION, IF IT CAN BE CONSIDERED AN OBJECTION GIVEN THAT IT'S IN IMPROPER FORM (CRC 3.1554), IS OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI; NOT REPORTED

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed May 24, 2016. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; Natasha Sumner of Dentons (415.267.4089) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1, 2, 3 and 4 and granted as to Issue 5. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues 3 and 4 (Negligence II, Loss of Consortium), defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant asserts this is the only way it attempted to sustain its initial burden. When read in conjunction with Mr. Thomas' deposition testimony, Mr. Trueblood's declaration does not demonstrate that Nor Cal employees never worked with, cut, installed and/or cleaned up asbestos-containing pipe insulation in the presence of Mr. Thomas. As to summary adjudication Issues 1 (Negligence I) and 2 (Strict Liability), defendant's motion is denied as moot as plaintiffs' did not allege these causes of action against defendant. As to summary adjudication Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant's motion is granted. Plaintiffs did assert a claim for punitive damages against defendant. (Complaint, 56:23-26.) The motion is granted pursuant to plaintiffs' representations that this inclusion was inadvertent. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Michele Lucas, CSR 4017 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Campbell Soup Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to defendant's argument regarding exposure, the Court interprets defendant's reply as withdrawing this issue. However, if defendant is moving forward with this issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant claims that it submitted "affirmative evidence that Campbell never exposed Plaintiff Howard Thomas to asbestos." The deposition testimony of Mr. Thomas on which defendant relies is insufficient to demonstrate that Campbell employees did not expose Mr. Thomas to asbestos-containing products or materials. Finally, even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, Mr. Baker's declaration creates a triable issue whether Campbell employees exposed Mr. Thomas to asbestos-containing pipe insulation. As to defendant's duty argument, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating that element of plaintiffs' negligence claim. The deposition testimony of Mr. Thomas and the declaration of Bob Cook do not demonstrate that defendant did not owe a duty to Mr. Thomas. They do not show that defendant did not know and could not have reasonably known that its employees' actions of sweeping up/cleaning up dust and debris could pose a health hazard to someone in the vicinity of that work. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, the declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen and the Safety Orders would create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Thomas. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Bob Cook are overruled. If the Court gets to the point of considering plaintiffs' evidence, defendant's objection to Dr. Cohen's declaration, if it can be considered an objection given that it's in improper form (CRC 3.1554), is overruled. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/GLW)
